label: Check eligibility
description: Check if users qualify for a service without requiring a full application or manual approval. Early eligibility checks reduce user drop-off later in the journey.
use_when: Eligibility rules exist and not all users will qualify.
Eligibility can be determined through questions, rules, or trusted data sources.
Services have multiple eligibility variants with different criteria.
do_not_use_when: All users are eligible by default.
Eligibility can only be confirmed after full submission and formal assessment.

building_blocks.notes: Required for automatic eligibility check linking data to the correct user.
Required when eligibility data is retrieved from external trusted sources.
Required when personal data from external sources is accessed.

steps.label: Explain eligibility requirements
steps.description: Tell users what the eligibility check involves and what information may be needed.

steps.label: Collect eligibility information (manual)
steps.description: Ask structured branching questions to determine eligibility manually.

steps.label: Automatic eligibility check
steps.description: Check eligibility using existing records or integrated data sources. Requires consent.

steps.label: Show eligibility outcome
steps.description: Tell users if they are eligible, not eligible, or if eligibility cannot be determined. Explain reason and next steps.

ux_considerations.text: Collect only information needed to reach an eligibility decision.
Make eligibility rules clear and consistent across all channels.
Support partial, conditional, and time-limited eligibility outcomes — avoid forcing a binary yes/no.

technical_notes.text: Eligibility rules must produce deterministic outcomes for the same input data.
